commit.message:
fix: embedded ICMP fabric redirect after NAT rewrite (split-RG)

In split-RG cluster, ICMP Time Exceeded from transit routers arrives
on the WAN node but must reach the LAN host on the peer node.
handle_embedded_icmp_v4/v6() was calling try_fabric_redirect() BEFORE
NAT rewrite — the peer received packets with SNAT'd embedded headers
and couldn't match them to any session, silently dropping them.

Fix: when FIB fails (UNREACHABLE/BLACKHOLE) for the original client,
set META_FLAG_KERNEL_ROUTE instead of attempting immediate fabric
redirect. xdp_forward's KERNEL_ROUTE path re-FIBs using post-NAT
packet headers (outer dst rewritten to original client IP), detects
UNREACHABLE, and fabric-redirects with correct headers.

Also change meta_flags assignment from = to |= to preserve
KERNEL_ROUTE flag when setting META_FLAG_EMBEDDED_ICMP.

Tested: split-RG MTR shows all intermediate hops (was 100% loss),
make test-failover 14/14 pass at 7.86 Gbps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add plan levels (Quick Fix, Standard, Complex)

Add three levels of planning:

- Quick Fix: < 1 hour, 2 min to create
- Standard: 1-3 days, 5-10 min to create
- Complex: > 1 week, full checklist

This prevents the "too much paperwork" problem while ensuring
important features get proper planning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(d2c): use PreparerFilingSignatureIndicator for self-filed FBAR XML

ThirdPartyPreparerIndicator="N" is invalid per XSD (ValidateIndicatorType
allows only "Y" or ""). D2C is self-filed, so we need
PreparerFilingSignatureIndicator="Y" instead, and omit
ThirdPartyPreparerIndicator entirely. This is likely why FinCEN sandbox
was silently rejecting our submissions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(bol-sync): use HTTP call instead of dynamic import for competitor analysis

Problem: Dynamic import of bol-sync-competitor-analysis.js failed in Vercel
serverless functions with deployment error.

Solution: Replace dynamic import with HTTP fetch to the competitor analysis
endpoint. Each Vercel API route is a separate serverless function and can't
directly import other functions.

Changes:
- Replace await import('./bol-sync-competitor-analysis.js') with fetch()
- Use VERCEL_URL env var to build API endpoint URL
- Add proper error handling for non-200 responses
- Maintain same response structure for backward compatibility

Result: Competitor analysis can now be triggered via syncType="competitor"
without deployment errors.

Fix GitHub OAuth: use localStorage instead of postMessage

GitHub redirect chain. Now the callback stores the token in

postMessage kept as backup.

commit.message:
Fix config import losing loops due to in-memory state overwrite

write_config() serializes loop_manager.to_dict() into device.json,
so importing a new loop_config into the dict isn't enough — the
in-memory LoopManager still has the old (empty) loops and overwrites
the imported data on the next write. Now reload loop_manager from
the imported config so both in-memory and on-disk state are consistent.

feat(phase5): multi-model agent support (M1)

AgentAdapter abstraction with Claude, Gemini, and Codex adapters.
Two-stage planning: Gemini proposes via pty session, Claude synthesizes
JSON plan. Fallback chain for Gemini models (3.1-pro → 3-pro → flash)
with capacity retry vs rate-limit distinction.

- adapters/: AgentAdapter base, ClaudeAdapter, GeminiAdapter, CodexAdapter, AdapterRegistry
- server.cjs: adapter registry init, spawnGeminiProposal, adapter-aware session mgmt
- jsonl-monitor.cjs: adapter-aware output parsing for non-Claude models
- prompts.cjs: multi-model planning prompt, Claude-only worker routing
- task-schema.cjs: model field validation on task + plan
- terminal-manager.cjs: adapter option on startSession/restoreSession
- Security: model name validation prevents shell injection in all adapters

commit.message:
fix(embedding): add document/query prefix for BGE-M3 NaN fix

Ollama BGE-M3 returns NaN embeddings for certain texts without prefix,
causing 500 errors and backfill job failure (0/113K processed).

- Add document_prefix/query_prefix to HttpProviderConfig
- Ollama/vLLM presets: "passage: " for documents, "query: " for queries
- EmbeddingProvider trait: embed_query() for search queries
- EmbeddingManager: embed_query() with separate cache namespace
- hybrid_search + Rhai db_hybrid_search use embed_query()

commit.message:
feat(forms): add server-driven forms with fromSchema() (#7)

* feat(forms): add server-driven forms with fromSchema()

Enable backends to send form definitions as JSON that the frontend
hydrates into fully functional forms. Includes schema validation,
validation descriptor resolution, effect handler currying, repeatable
support, and comprehensive error reporting via SchemaValidationError.

Key fixes included:
- Invalid regex patterns now throw SchemaValidationError (not SyntaxError)
- Negative min/max values on repeatables are rejected during validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(forms): remove arbitrary 3-field-per-row limit

The limit was a surface-level builder guard — neither the type system
(FormFieldRow.fields: FormFieldConfig[]) nor the renderer (FormRow.tsx)
imposed it. Removing it fixes a silent bug in fromSchema() where >3
fields were auto-split into separate rows without matching the schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(forms): add comprehensive e2e tests for fromSchema()

30 tests covering rendering, validation, conditions, effects,
repeatables, submission, and a full integration signup form scenario.

fix: 修復 TypeError: Cannot set properties of null (v1.1.2)

根因：chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true }) 可能回傳
空陣列，導致 activeTab 為 undefined，初始化中斷，後續 DOM 操作
對 null 元素設定 textContent 而報錯。

修復：
- 初始化加上 try/catch 與多重 fallback（activeTab → lastFocused window）
- getElementById 結果全部加 null check
- refreshOpenGroups / refreshClosedGroups 加 try/catch
- showToast 加 null check

chore: upgrade all dependencies

- langchain-core 1.2.7 → 1.2.17 (SSRF fix)

commit.message:
fix(bol-sync): resolve competitor analysis timeout by deprecating extended sync

Problem: Extended sync (syncType='extended') was executing competitor analysis
synchronously within bol-sync-trigger.ts, exceeding Vercel's 10-minute timeout.

Solution:
- Removed 140 lines of synchronous competitor code from extended case
- Replaced with deprecation notice directing to 'competitor' sync type
- Competitor sync already uses dedicated endpoint (bol-sync-competitor-analysis.ts)
- Updated frontend to remove "Extended Data" button, renumber phases
- Removed 'extended' from BolSyncType definition

Changes:
- api/bol-sync-trigger.ts: Deprecated 'extended', updated JSDoc
- src/lib/bol-api.ts: Removed 'extended' from BolSyncType
- src/pages/BolDashboard.tsx: Removed extended from UI, state, and runAllPhases()

Result: No more timeouts. Competitor analysis runs via dedicated endpoint
with proper timeout handling. TypeScript clean (npm run typecheck passes).

commit.message:
Fix quick-claim saving: append new entry if IP not yet in ipData

handleSaveItem used map() which only updates existing entries — a newly
claimed free IP was never in ipData so the save was silently dropped.
Now checks if the IP exists first; appends as a new entry if not found.

commit.message:
Track E: Refactor decompressZstd to well-founded recursion for spec proofs (#744)

* refactor: replace decompressZstd while-loop with well-founded recursion

The `while pos < data.size` loop in `decompressZstd` compiles to an
opaque `Lean.Loop.forIn` that cannot be unfolded in proofs. Extract the
loop body into `decompressZstdWF` using explicit recursion with
`termination_by data.size - pos`, following the same pattern as
`decompressBlocksWF`. The public `decompressZstd` API is unchanged —
it now delegates to `decompressZstdWF data 0 ByteArray.empty`.

This unblocks future spec work: position advancement, output
concatenation, and end-to-end decompression correctness theorems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for decompressZstd WF refactoring

commit.message:
feat: enforce "less is more" design principle with minimal default tokens

Reduce over-specified design tokens to minimum viable defaults:
- Border radius: 6 → 3 levels (Default, Large, Full)
- Spacing: 6 → 4 levels (S, M, L, XL)
- Shadows: 5 → 3 levels (None, Default, Large)
- Font weights: 4 → 2 (Regular, Semibold)
- Type scale: 7 → 5 (Display, H1, H2, Body, Small)
- Button sizes: keep 3 (Small, Default, Large)

Each dimension includes guidance to add more levels via `iterate` when needed.

fix: 修復收合/展開全部與升序/降序功能 (v1.1.1)

Bug 1 — 收合/展開全部沒有正確執行：
- 根因：chrome.windows.getCurrent() 在 popup 中可能取得錯誤 windowId
  導致 chrome.tabGroups.query({ windowId }) 查不到群組
- 修復：改用 chrome.tabs.query({ active: true, currentWindow: true })
  取得可靠的 windowId，並加上 fallback 機制
- 額外修復：收合全部時先將 active tab 移出群組，避免 Chrome
  拒絕收合含 active tab 的群組
- 修正 toast 訊息（收合/展開不再顯示「排序完成」）

Bug 2 — 升序/降序功能有誤：
- 同一排序方式再點一次會自動切換升降序方向
- 升序/降序按鈕改為 async，確保排序完成後再更新 UI

commit.message:
Track E: Prove resolveSingleFseTable RLE and repeat mode position specs (#740)

* feat: Prove resolveSingleFseTable RLE and repeat mode position specs

Add two theorems completing position tracking for 3 of 4 compression
modes in resolveSingleFseTable:
- resolveSingleFseTable_rle_pos: position advances by exactly 1
- resolveSingleFseTable_repeat_pos: position is unchanged

Combined with the existing predefined_pos theorem, only the FSE
compressed mode remains unspecified for position tracking.

Closes #736

fix: use postinstall for prisma generate on Vercel

Vercel's Next.js builder runs next build directly, bypassing package.json
build scripts. Moving prisma generate to postinstall ensures the Prisma
client is regenerated with AI schema enums before the build starts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use deterministic URLs for VITE_API_URL like the working backup

The backup repo (integrity-home-healthcare) hardcoded VITE_API_URL as
https://<slug>-care-api.onrender.com in render.yaml — no API calls,
no race conditions. Our deploy service was creating the frontend with
empty envVars then trying to set VITE_API_URL after the build started.

Now: construct URLs deterministically from the service name (same
pattern as render.yaml.template), pass all env vars at service
creation time. No post-creation updates, no delays, no redeployments.
Also sets FRONTEND_URL on the backend at creation time.
